name: Baton Image Builder
description: Container image building for Baton services. Detects project type (Python or Node.js), generates Dockerfiles, builds and pushes Docker images, and manages image metadata.

types.name: ImageBuilder
types.description: Build and manage container images for circuit nodes.

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Initialize ImageBuilder with project directory and optional circuit name

inputs.name: circuit_name
inputs.description: Name of the circuit for image tagging

postconditions: _project_dir is set to Path(project_dir)
_circuit_name is set to circuit_name parameter
side_effects: Converts project_dir to Path object and stores in instance

functions.name: detect_runtime
functions.description: Detect the runtime type of a service directory by checking for package.json (Node.js) or defaulting to Python

inputs.name: service_dir
inputs.description: Service directory to detect runtime for

postconditions: Returns 'node' if package.json exists in service_dir
Returns 'python' otherwise

functions.name: generate_dockerfile
functions.description: Generate a Dockerfile for a node's service based on detected runtime, creates dockerfile directory if needed, and writes Dockerfile to .baton/dockerfiles/

error_cases.name: FileSystemError
error_cases.condition: Unable to create dockerfiles directory or write Dockerfile

error_cases.name: JSONDecodeError
error_cases.condition: package.json exists but contains invalid JSON (for Node detection)

postconditions: Dockerfile is created at .baton/dockerfiles/Dockerfile.{node_name}
Returns Path to created Dockerfile
Dockerfile content matches detected runtime (Python or Node.js)
side_effects: Creates .baton/dockerfiles directory if it doesn't exist
Logs info message about generated Dockerfile

structured_side_effects.description: Reads service directory to detect runtime and entry points

functions.name: build
functions.description: Build a Docker image for a node's service by generating Dockerfile if needed, running docker build, extracting digest, and saving image info

error_cases.name: DockerBuildFailure
error_cases.condition: Docker build command returns non-zero exit code

error_cases.name: FileSystemError
error_cases.condition: Unable to generate Dockerfile or save image info

preconditions: Docker daemon is running and accessible
service_dir exists and contains valid service files
postconditions: Docker image is built with specified tag
ImageInfo is returned with node_name, tag, built_at timestamp, and digest
Image metadata is saved to .baton/images.json
side_effects: Logs info message about built image

structured_side_effects.description: May generate Dockerfile if not exists

functions.name: push
functions.description: Push a Docker image to its registry using docker push command

error_cases.name: DockerPushFailure
error_cases.condition: Docker push command returns non-zero exit code

preconditions: Image with specified tag exists locally
User is authenticated to target registry
postconditions: Image is pushed to registry
Returns the pushed tag
side_effects: Logs info message about pushed image

functions.name: list_images
functions.description: List all built images from .baton/images.json file

error_cases.name: JSONDecodeError
error_cases.condition: images.json exists but contains invalid JSON

error_cases.name: FileReadError
error_cases.condition: images.json exists but cannot be read

postconditions: Returns list of ImageInfo objects from images.json
Returns empty list if images.json doesn't exist

functions.name: _save_image_info
functions.description: Save or update image info in .baton/images.json, updating existing entry for same node_name or appending new entry
postconditions: Image info is persisted to .baton/images.json
Existing entry with same node_name is updated
New entry is appended if node_name not found
side_effects: Writes updated image list to images.json

functions.name: _detect_python_entry
functions.description: Detect Python entry point by parsing pyproject.toml [project.scripts] section or checking for common entry files (app.py, main.py, app/__init__.py)

inputs.name: service_dir
inputs.description: Service directory to detect Python entry point in

error_cases.name: FileReadError
error_cases.condition: pyproject.toml exists but cannot be read

postconditions: Returns command list for Python entry point
Defaults to ['python', '-m', 'app'] if no entry point detected

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and checks for Python entry points

functions.name: _detect_node_entry
functions.description: Detect Node.js entry point by parsing package.json for scripts.start or main field, or checking for common entry files (index.js, server.js, app.js)

inputs.name: service_dir
inputs.description: Service directory to detect Node.js entry point in

error_cases.name: JSONDecodeError
error_cases.condition: package.json exists but contains invalid JSON

error_cases.name: FileReadError
error_cases.condition: package.json exists but cannot be read

postconditions: Returns entry file name for Node.js
Defaults to 'index.js' if no entry point detected

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and checks for Node.js entry points

invariants: _PYTHON_DOCKERFILE uses python:3.12-slim base image and exposes port 8080
_NODE_DOCKERFILE uses node:20-slim base image and exposes port 8080
All Dockerfiles set WORKDIR to /app and PORT env to 8080
Runtime detection: package.json presence indicates Node.js, otherwise Python
